properties.remark: HAIL BETWEEN 1 AND 1.5 INCHES. WHITEOUT OF HAIL REPORTED AT THE WAL-MART.
ACCOMPANIED BY STRONG WINDS.

ESTIMATED BETWEEN NICKEL AND QUARTER SIZE.
LARGE POPLAR TREE DOWNED ACROSS HIGHWAY 107.
SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS BROKEN. ROTTED TREES DOWNED.
HAIL MEASURING UP TO 1 INCH IN DIAMETER FELL BETWEEN 649 P.M. AND 654 P.M. EDT
8 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS KNOCKED DOWN. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 55 MPH.
NUMEROUS LARGE TREES DOWN...SOME AS LARGE AS 24 INCHES IN DIAMETER. ALSO SOME SLIGHT DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES FROM DOWNED TREES.

2 LARGE TRESS DOWN ACROSS BOND FALLS ROAD EAST OF PAULDING.
TREES DOWN AROUND 8 TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER. ADDITIONAL TREES DOWN ON HWY 16...WHICH WERE 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
TREES DOWN AROUND CABLE LAKE.

POSSIBLE TORNADO ACCORDING TO TWO PUBLIC REPORTS. TORNADO LASTED A FEW MINUTES. UPROOTED LARGE TREES...PLYWOOD WENT THROUGH A MOTORHOME AND PORTION STILL STUCK IN THE MOTORHOME...PORTION OF A TRAILER ROOF RIPPED OFF.
18 TO 20 INCH DIAMETER SPRUCE TREE DOWN AND A 10 INCH DIAMETER CEDAR TREE DOWN.

LARGE BRANCHES DOWN

SEVERAL TREES AND MANY BRANCHES DOWN. ONE TREE WAS GREATER THAN ONE FOOT IN DIAMETER. ALSO LARGE POPLAR TREE SNAPPED.
